i.
i qr rd'90
Senator David Roberti
PRESIDtrNT PRO TEMPORE, CALIFORNIA STATE SENATE
April 5, 1990
Kathyrn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street 
Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca. 95814
Dear Ms. Donovan:
lam requesting a formal written opinion from the FPPC to ascertain
whether ballot measure committees controlled by Senator David
Roberti may receive contributions from campaign committees controlled
by other officeholders or candidates for elective office.
It is my understanding that the FPPC has instructed staff to develop
regulations to determine when expenditure activities of a ballot measure
committee are deemed to so benefit a candidate that the expenditures
become contributions to the candidate.
It is also our understanding that the Commission has already instructed
the FPPC staff to modify the Hiltachk Advice Letter to allow candi-
dates to transfer funds to another candidaters controlled ballot measure
committee.
Senator Roberti controls the following ballot measure committees:
l. House the Homeless, A Committee for Proposition 107:
Senator David Roberti, Chair 
l.D. 891049
2. Californians Against Reapportionment Dishonesty:
David Roberti, Chair 
l.D. 890905
3. Senator David Roberti Ballot Measure Committee l.D. 892004
Your prompt response to this request would be appreciated.
Treasurer
Campaign Finance Office . 11785 Laurelcrest Drive o Studio City, California 916O4 . (215) 877-5855
NOT PRINTED AT PUtsLIC EXPENSE
.@, 
ror

sections.question: Whether ballot measure committees controlled by Senator David Roberti may receive contributions from campaign committees controlled by other officeholders or candidates for elective office.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether ballot measure committees controlled by Senator Roberti can accept contributions from other officeholders' or candidates' campaign committees, and references FPPC staff work on regulations regarding when ballot measure expenditures benefit candidates enough to constitute contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or formal opinion section present. Document references three ballot measure committees controlled by Senator Roberti and mentions prior FPPC work on related regulations and advice letters.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the Campaign Finance Office on behalf of Senator David Roberti seeking a formal written FPPC opinion on whether his controlled ballot measure committees may receive contributions from campaign committees of other officeholders or candidates.
